report_name: Russia Ramps Up Cyber Espionage to Steal Western Tech Amid Sanctions, EU Officials Warn

threat_actors.name: Russian Intelligence Agencies
threat_actors.description: Overarching Russian state intelligence services conducting a whole-of-government cyber espionage campaign to acquire Western technology, defense secrets, and conduct reconnaissance against critical infrastructure to circumvent international sanctions.
threat_actors.goals: Acquire Western technology and defense secrets to support Russia's military-industrial complex and wartime economy, and conduct reconnaissance for potential future disruptive attacks.

threat_actors.name: APT28
threat_actors.description: Mentioned as a known Russian state-sponsored hacking group involved in extensive reconnaissance and initial access operations as part of Russia's broader cyber espionage campaign.

threat_actors.name: APT29
threat_actors.description: Mentioned as a known Russian state-sponsored hacking group involved in extensive reconnaissance and initial access operations as part of Russia's broader cyber espionage campaign.

attack_patterns.name: Gather Victim Identity Information
attack_patterns.description: Russian APTs conduct extensive reconnaissance to identify key personnel and systems within target organizations in the defense and technology sectors.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Spear-phishing is cited as a primary initial access vector for Russian state-sponsored groups to gain a foothold in target networks.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Local System
attack_patterns.description: After gaining access, threat actors focus on collecting sensitive data from local systems, including design documents, research data, and strategic plans.

attack_patterns.name: Data Destruction
attack_patterns.description: The warning about reconnaissance for future attacks on critical infrastructure points to an intent to use destructive capabilities, similar to past attacks involving malware like NotPetya or Industroyer.

attack_patterns.name: Trusted Relationship
attack_patterns.description: Russian intelligence uses front companies and middlemen, abusing trusted business relationships to circumvent sanctions and acquire technology.

attack_patterns.name: Domain Trust Discovery
attack_patterns.description: A hunting hint suggests looking for the execution of 'nltest /domain_trusts' to detect internal reconnaissance of Active Directory trusts.

attack_patterns.name: Brute Force
attack_patterns.description: A hunting hint suggests monitoring for brute-force or password spray attacks against external-facing VPN services as a common initial access technique.

malware.name: NotPetya
malware.description: Mentioned as a past example of destructive malware used in attacks, indicating potential future intent by Russian actors.

malware.name: Industroyer
malware.description: Mentioned as a past example of destructive malware used against critical infrastructure, indicating potential future intent by Russian actors.

tools.name: Mimikatz
tools.description: Mentioned as a credential harvesting tool whose usage should be monitored for with behavioral detection.

tools.name: PsExec
tools.description: Mentioned as a lateral movement tool whose usage should be monitored for with behavioral detection.

tools.name: Nltest
tools.description: The command 'nltest /domain_trusts' was cited as a hunting hint for detecting internal reconnaissance of Active Directory domain trusts.

identities.name: European defense companies
identities.description: A class of organizations in Europe targeted by Russian cyber espionage for intellectual property and strategic plans.

identities.name: European technology companies
identities.description: A class of organizations in Europe targeted by Russian cyber espionage for technology theft to circumvent sanctions.

identities.name: European critical infrastructure
identities.description: A class of organizations in Europe, particularly the energy sector, being probed by Russian cyber actors for reconnaissance for potential future disruptive or destructive attacks.

identities.name: Swedish power plant
identities.description: Cited as the target of an unsuccessful attack, as evidence of Russia's intent to conduct reconnaissance for destructive attacks against critical infrastructure.

infrastructure.name: Russian APT C2 Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Known C2 infrastructure associated with Russian APT groups, which organizations are advised to monitor for traffic.

infrastructure.name: Front Companies for Sanctions Evasion
infrastructure.description: Fake companies established in neutral or friendly countries like Turkey to act as intermediaries for procuring sanctioned goods, such as machine tools.

indicators.name: Russian APT AD Domain Trust Discovery
indicators.pattern:
title: Russian APT AD Domain Trust Discovery
id: 2b1c6d5e-8f9a-4b7c-9d8e-1a2b3c4d5e6f
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of nltest to enumerate domain trusts, a common reconnaissance technique for Russian APTs mentioned as a hunting hint.
references:
  - https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQFb0VQjszGREt8dusZ62IO6IQ5Tq6qvMrfc3meYgKp_QYPFRRInzCA878eOpCczHoO-g5bo3DOm4V-6ovfDAvfwCQ7zClb5SZhAQ66mGO1CzVWO-90eKvIp27IPaE6cJoAsWuP1yajcQqx4MNtO7A_LgEoM6tP1dN9_hFqyEgt0iqlMGJpjd5AzxkoNV12GKjYNypMdPTNHv7l2sH0r2hNy2T8P1Ddvz7qxbWe6ho2OCDHi
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-30
tags:
  - attack.t1482
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\nltest.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: '/domain_trusts'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of 'nltest /domain_trusts' for Active Directory reconnaissance, a TTP noted in the article's hunting hints for Russian state actors.

indicators.name: Detection of Common Russian APT Tools
indicators.pattern:
title: Detection of Common Russian APT Tools
id: 8a7b6c5d-4e3f-4a9b-8c1d-2e3f4a5b6c7d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of tools like Mimikatz or PsExec, which are commonly used by Russian APTs for credential harvesting and lateral movement as mentioned in the report.
references:
  - https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQFb0VQjszGREt8dusZ62IO6IQ5Tq6qvMrfc3meYgKp_QYPFRRInzCA878eOpCczHoO-g5bo3DOm4V-6ovfDAvfwCQ7zClb5SZhAQ66mGO1CzVWO-90eKvIp27IPaE6cJoAsWuP1yajcQqx4MNtO7A_LgEoM6tP1dN9_hFqyEgt0iqlMGJpjd5AzxkoNV12GKjYNypMdPTNHv7l2sH0r2hNy2T8P1Ddvz7qxbWe6ho2OCDHi
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-30
tags:
  - attack.t1003
  - attack.t1021.002
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\mimikatz.exe'
      - '\PsExec.exe'
      - '\PsExec64.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of Mimikatz or PsExec, which the article recommends monitoring for as signs of Russian APT activity.

relationships.source_name: Russian APT AD Domain Trust Discovery
Detection of Common Russian APT Tools
